name: Beer Cheese
recipeIngredient: 1 8oz package processed Swiss or American cheese
5 cups bread or all purpose flour (approx.)
8 oz natural Swiss cheese, cut into 1/4 inch cubes
recipeInstructions:
1 Combine the beer, water, sugar, salt, butter, and processed cheese in a large saucepan. Heat until hot.
2 Remove from heat and cool to warm.
3 In a large mixing bowl, combine 2 cups flour and yeast. Pour in the warm cheese mixture and beat with mixer flat beater for 3 minutes.
4 Stir in the remaining flour, half cup at a time. Switch to dough hook when the dough becomes too heavy for the flat beater.
5 Add cheese bits while mixer is running. Knead for 10 minutes.
6 Place dough in buttered bowl and turn over to coat ball completely. Cover with plastic wrap until dough has risen to twice its size, about 1 hour.
7 Punch down and knead for 30 seconds to deflate. Divide dough into half. Roll each half into a 12x6 inch rectangle.
8 Cut each rectangle lengthwise into three 2-inch strips, leaving them joined at one end by 1/2 inch piece. Braid the strips. Tuck under ends to make dough about same length as pan.
9 Place in bake pan.
10 Cover with waxed paper and let rise in warm place until dough has risen about 1/2 inch above pan, about 45 minutes.
11 Preheat oven to 350 degrees. Bake for about 45 minutes.
12 Remove from oven and place on rack to cool.
Makes: 2 medium loaves in 8x4 inch loaf pans.
